source_jobs.title: Personal Stylist (Remote CA, WA, NV, TX, OH, GA, FL, NC, SC)
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   


THE ROLE:

We're looking for stylists who take their craft seriously. A successful Short Story stylist has a keen eye for style, figure flattering silhouettes, attention to detail, and great communication skills. You'll be helping clients with your creativity, knowledge, and thoughtfulness. You should be able to recommend outfits and recognize what works well together and what doesn't. You care about getting it right, not just getting it done. Our clients are at the core of everything we do, and that should be reflected in your work.




DAY TO DAY:

 - Use our platform to style clients each week

 - Curate well-coordinated boxes of styles to perfectly fit our clients

 - Actively incorporate client feedback to improve recommendations

 - Communicate directly with clients and own the development of your relationships

 - Write thoughtful stylist notes to guide your clients through their petite style journey

 - Meet quality and efficiency metrics while managing your own schedule




ROLE REQUIREMENTS:

 - Must reside in California, Nevada, Texas, Ohio, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina or South Carolina (see full location exclusions below)

 - Must be currently authorized to work in the US

 - At least 1 year of experience as a virtual stylist or other comparable online styling experience

 - Must be able to work 25-29 hours per week




WHO YOU ARE:

 - Experienced. You've worked as a virtual or online stylist before, or have a strong background in fashion with a genuine eye for style.

 - Customer-obsessed. You think about the person on the other end of every box.

 - Tech-savvy. You're comfortable working in digital platforms and learning new tools quickly.

 - Reliable. You show up, hit your commitments, and manage your time without being managed.

 - A strong writer. Your stylist notes are clear, warm, and specific, not generic.



Hours & Scheduling
We offer two scheduling options to fit your lifestyle. We are looking for Stylists who can commit to one of the following:

 - Option 1: 20–25 hours per week

 - Option 2: 25–29 hours per week
   
   Please note: Weekly hours may occasionally flex within your chosen option’s range based on business needs.


Compensation & Details

 - Position Type: Part-time, fully remote

 - Pay Range: $15.00–$17.50/hour (depending on location)

 - Growth: Opportunities to advance as you grow with the team




WHAT TO EXPECT IN THE HIRING PROCESS:

 - Application

 - Stylist Assessment

 - First Interview (video)

 - Second Interview (video)

 - Offer



Location Exclusions

We are unable to hire in the following locations:

 - California: San Francisco, West Hollywood

 - Washington: Seattle, SeaTac, Bellingham, Burien, Everett, King County unincorporated, Renton, Tukwila

Short Story is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and a work en

source_jobs.title: Fulfillment Associate
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/2d3b8476-7238-4047-a89d-dc54c1b03847/85b1447e-6a8c-45e2-b51a-cdaa8516b9df/team.jpg]




THE ROLE:

We're looking for motivated, hardworking, and reliable Retail Fulfillment Associates to join our warehouse team! In this role, you will be responsible for daily warehouse operations, including picking and packing orders, processing incoming returns, and organizing new inventory. We are looking for people with strong work ethic and effective communication skills. 

Responsibilities:

 - Pick and pack customer orders accurately using an order list.

 - Fold and prep clothing professionally to ensure a beautiful unboxing experience.

 - Tag and organize incoming merchandise quickly and accurately.

 - Process customer returns and assist with basic administrative warehouse tasks.

 - Maintain a clean, safe, and organized warehouse environment.

Hours:

 - Monday-Friday 6:00am - 2:30pm (8 hour shift with 30 minute lunch break)

 - Location: South San Francisco (near South San Francisco Caltrain station)

 - We’re looking for people who can start immediately

Qualifications and Skills:

 - Experience working in apparel retail or warehouse operations preferred

 - Sense of urgency - this is a fast paced startup environment

 - Excellent attendance, punctuality, and accountability.

 - Effective communication skills and a positive, can-do attitude.

 - Reliability and accountability

 - Consistently meeting goals and metrics

 - Ability to travel to our South SF warehouse Monday-Friday

 - Ability to be on your feet 8-10 hours a day (stand, walk, push, pull, squat, bend, and reach), climb and descend stairs safely, and lift up to 50 pounds.

Why work for Short Story?

 - Great Benefits: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage.

 - 401k plan with company match

 - Paid time off for vacation (full-time employees only), paid sick days, and paid holidays

 - Career Growth: Opportunities to progress rapidly within a fast-growing startup alongside an experienced founding team.

 - Team Perks: Fully stocked office snacks, catered team lunch every Friday, and an energetic, diverse, and passionate work environment.

 - Work for a mission-driven company in an energetic work environment with passionate, diverse, and accomplished team members.

Short Story is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and a work environment free of discrimination and harassment for all employees and applicants. We work diligently to ensure equal opportunity for all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, age, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

We’re hiring Customer Support Associates to join our CX team. Our ideal candidate is someone who is passionate about startups, ecommerce, consumer brands, and emerging technologies with a focus on doing the right thing for people, clients, and the world.

We want only the most proactive, empathetic team members who are great problem solvers and dedicated to delivering the best client experience. Our focus is on finding people who share our values and can help keep our service top-notch.



About the Role:

As part of the Client Experience team, you will ensure our clients have a smooth, delightful experience.



You Will Be Responsible For:
- CX Support: Responding to and managing client experience inquiries in our various channels
- Ad-Hoc Tasks: Complete ongoing clerical, administrative, and routine tasks to keep our day to day client experience operations running smoothly
- Data Analytics: Track performance on a daily, weekly, and monthly basis by assisting with the collection of data and explaining the “why” of what happens
- User Interviews: Facilitate regular interviews with users to collect feedback and insights to better serve our audience



Qualifications:
- 2+ years of experience in customer service
- Fluent English (C1 or C2 Proficiency)
- Perfect writing 
- Familiarity with Slack, Google Sheets
- Extreme attention to detail
- Previous experience working in ecommerce or a tech startup is a plus
- Must be flexible and able to wear many hats
- Able to translate client experience into recommendations



Schedule and Location:
- 40 hours a week



Job Type
- Full-time, contract

Short Story is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and a work environment free of discrimination and harassment for all employees and applicants. We work diligently to ensure equal opportunity for all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, age, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Full Stack (US, Canada, SF)
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

At Short Story, you will be a part of a high-velocity engineering team that creates new user experiences, optimizes warehouse and logistics operations, and improves data insights to better serve our customers. We are looking for self-motivated individuals who enjoy tackling ambiguity and can solve complex problems end-to-end. 



The Role:

As an early Senior Full Stack Engineer, you will build internal and customer-facing services for processing customer orders and establish key design patterns/guidelines for all backend workloads.

(Enjoy playing Factorio? Let’s chat!)



Must Haves:

 - 5+ years of software development experience, 2+ Years of Python experience

 - Familiar with infrastructure, CI/CD practices

 - Familiar with the following technologies:
   
   - A Python web framework 
   
   - A testing framework (PyTest preferred)
   
   - A cloud provider (AWS preferred)
   
   - Containerized workloads (Kubernetes preferred)
   
   - Relational database (Postgres preferred) and SQL 

 - Focus on communication and documentation

 - Familiar with common API and backend testing patterns



Nice to Haves:

 - Worked in a fully remote environment with colleagues from around the world

 - Test suite/pattern creation and automation experience 

 - Managed an engineering team of 3+ people

 - Experience with queue / event-driven systems

 - Worked in sprint-based environments with an OKR framework



Compensation & Benefits:

 - Generous Paid Time Off

 - Comprehensive health, vision, and dental care

 - 401k with company match (US)

 - Either SF, or remote in US or Canada

 - Employee discount on merchandise

 - Opportunity to work at a Y-Combinator backed startup alongside a dedicated and experienced founding team

Short Story is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and a work environment free of discrimination and harassment for all employees and applicants. We work diligently to ensure equal opportunity for all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, age, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

source_jobs.title: Founding Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

The Role

Startups win by having great people. We're looking for a Founding Recruiter who'll lead all things recruiting. Not someone who just fills positions, but someone who thinks about what kind of team we need, and how to build it.

 - You'll work directly with the CEO to find the best people to join us across Operations, Styling, Product, Engineering, Marketing, Client Experience, and more.

 - The best talent people don't just follow a process—they invent it. You'll decide what "good" means, how we find those people, and how we close them.

 - This is the kind of role where you'll look back years from now and see your fingerprints on the entire company. This is an opportunity to be the architect behind a high-performing, mission-driven team at one of the fastest-growing ecommerce startups.


This role is full-time and onsite in our SF office.


Qualifications

 - You can operate as team of one—rolling up your sleeves to source, coordinate, and close. You'll work closely with the CEO and HR Manager, but you'll be the one figuring out what needs to be done and doing it.

 - You think like a founder: high judgment, bias for action, and ownership mentality.

 - You write outbound messages that actually get responses and know how to search for the right people in non-obvious places.

 - You're smart with tools - Ashby, Linkedin, and using AI to automate the repetitive parts.

 - 7+ years of experience in recruiting, with at least 3 years in a startup or high-growth environment.

Responsibilities

 - Full Cycle Recruiting: Own the full-cycle recruiting across roles

 - Talent Strategy: Work with founders and hiring managers to set hiring goals, scope new roles and develop talent roadmaps

 - Sourcing: Build and run outbound sourcing strategies to reach top talent

 - Manage Talent Infrastructure: Including Ashby, LinkedIn Recruiter, and hiring data

 - Growth Strategy: Inform founders on team composition and organizational scaling

 - Talent Operations: Own candidate coordination, recruiting tools, and continuously improve our hiring processes and structures

 - Brand Building: Build out Short Story's brand and values with authenticity, empathy, and clarity in every candidate interaction
   

What We Value

 - An obsession with detail - every word, every punctuation mark

 - Excellent writing skills

 - Anything is possible mindset

 - An extremely strong work ethic with commitment to quality and detail

 - Scrappiness
   

Why You Should Join Us

 - Strong backing from investors YCombinator, Tribe Capital

 - Best-in-class team that is deeply mission-aligned

 - Have a huge impact on a small team with lots of runway for growth

 - Competitive salary and equity compensation

 - Excellent benefits and versatile health, dental, and vision coverage plans

 - Paid parental leave

 - Unlimited

source_jobs.title: Senior Webflow Developer & CRO Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

About the Role:

We’re looking for someone who is obsessed with creating a constantly evolving and improving website experience. You are a master of implementation and strategic thinking and can put yourself in a customer’s shoes to understand what motivates them to purchase

In this role, you will be responsible for building, testing, and improving the site to drive growth. The Senior Webflow Developer & CRO Specialist will work closely with design, brand, and growth to continuously improve our online experience.

You'll be deep in Webflow every day, translating designs into high-fidelity pages, running experiments, and continuously finding ways to improve conversion performance. You take pride in clean, precise implementation and you bring a CRO mindset to everything you build — not just executing what you're told, but identifying what to test and why.




What you'll do:

Website Development & Webflow Ownership

 - Own the implementation and ongoing maintenance of our Webflow-hosted website

 - Work directly with design to build engaging web content

 - Build systematic, reusable components aligned with our design system

 - Ensure pages are responsive, fast, and production-ready across all devices

 - Support landing pages, redesigns, and iterative improvements

 - Ensure pages are properly structured for search engine crawlability and indexing (meta tags, page hierarchy, schema basics)

CRO Strategy & Experimentation

 - Own the CRO experimentation roadmap — identifying opportunities, forming hypotheses, and prioritizing tests

 - Implement A/B tests and CRO experiments end-to-end

 - Ensure test variants are isolated, stable, and correctly deployed

 - Validate that experiment events and conversion tracking fire correctly

 - Analyze results, draw conclusions, and iterate

Performance Monitoring & QA

 - Monitor page performance, usability, and conversion behavior

 - Catch issues and regressions before they become problems

 - Maintain high-quality standards for everything that ships

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Partner with Growth to align experimentation priorities with business goals

 - Collaborate closely with Design to bring their vision to life accurately

 - Communicate tradeoffs and constraints clearly

What We're Looking For:

 - 3–6 years of hands-on Webflow experience — you can build complex, scalable pages with confidence and speed

 - Experience in conversion rate optimization, growth, digital product optimization, or related performance marketing roles, ideally within eCommerce or mobile environments

 - Strong analytical foundation with hands-on experience designing, launching, and analyzing A/B and multivariate tests

 - Demonstrated success improving conversion rate, revenue per visitor, average order value, or other core performance metrics

 - Proficiency

source_jobs.title: Merchandise Associate (International Only)
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

Short Story is seeking a dedicated, detail oriented Merchandise Associate to join our dynamic team. We are looking for someone who thrives on absolute data integrity to own the end-to-end creation and maintenance of our items and POs. Because you will manage our operational source of truth, an eagle eye for detail is essential for this role. You are someone who catches a misplaced digit or an incorrect size tag from a mile away, and you take genuine pride in protecting the accuracy of our systems. Working remotely during active US business hours, you will ensure our entire styling pipeline runs on precise, highly reliable backend information.

What You'll Do:

 - Product Catalog Maintenance: Collaborate with the Merchandise Team to update and maintain the product catalog, ensuring product descriptions, images, and other relevant information are current and accurate.

 - Item Set Up: Accurately enter product information and pricing into our database system. Ensure all data is up-to-date and error-free.

 - Data Entry: Accurately enter product information into our database system. Ensure all data is up-to-date and error-free.

 - Quality Control: Conduct regular quality checks on product data to ensure accuracy and consistency. Identify and address any discrepancies or issues promptly.

 - Communication: Maintain open and clear communication with the merchandise team in San Francisco. Provide regular updates on tasks, projects, and any challenges encountered.

 - Ad Hoc Support: Provide support to the merchandise team as needed, including assisting with special projects, conducting research, and performing other administrative tasks.
   

What We’re Looking For

 - A Bachelor’s degree in Clothing Technology, Apparel Engineering, or Fashion Merchandising.

 - 2 to 6+ years of experience working inside a structured, apparel operation—specifically within buying, merchandise planning, or brand management roles

 - Fluent in English

 - 2+ years of experience with data entry in English

 - Exceptional writing skills

 - Extreme attention to detail

 - Strong with Data: comfort running calculations, analyzing data, and tracking numbers under tight deadlines.

 - Proficiency with G Suite (Google Sheets, Docs, Slides)

 - Flexible and able to wear many hats

Bonus Qualifications:

 - Familiarity with Slack and Airtable

 - Previous experience working in e-commerce tech startup

 - You thrive in fast-moving environments where we move quickly

 - You are energetic and ready to be on-the-go

 - Eager to learn and excited to help build a company from the ground up

Schedule and Location:

 - 100% Remote (Based in the Philippines)

 - This will be a Full-Time, 40 hours/week role (may include some weekend and evening hours)

 - Aligned with US business hours (Night shift PHT).

Pay Rate:

 - PHP 48,000-7

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager of Technical Design
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

The Role:

The Senior Manager of Technical Design will lead our growing team of technical designers and drive the development of market-leading petite fashion. Using data and technology along with craft and talent, this role will innovate our technical design and product creation processes. This role is about setting standards, refining fit and quality metrics, building a team of sterling experts and making our workflows the most efficient they can be. Collaborating closely with different teams and with vendors, this role will shape product strategies that strengthen Short Story’s foothold as the market leader of fit and quality in petite fashion. Talent development and transparent communication are integral to leadership, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and collaboration across the organization.



Responsibilities:

 - Lead the growing Technical Design team at Story Story, specializing in petite fit expertise.

 - Establish processes to maintain Short Story’s  industry-leading position in fit and quality.

 - Educate and coach the Technical Design team to enhance their skills and knowledge. 

 - Evaluate and stabilize processes and tools in order to execute product strategies efficiently (from fit blocks and grade rules to WIP and tech pack excellence).

 - Drive innovation in our technical design work, establishing best in class tools and resources to execute against product vision.

 - Streamline development process, ultimately driving speed, bandwidth and accuracy;  build processes that are easily scaled.

 - Champion for product excellence in fit and quality; delivering products that delight clients (in fit, style and quality).

 - Hold internal and external partners accountable for excellence across all fit and quality metrics.

 - Partner with vendors to ensure products meet fit goals and quality standards in bulk production.

 - Partner with product development and merchandising to achieve process and performance goals.

 - Foster strong vendor co-creation partnerships with processes and clear standards that are both client-centric and efficient.

 - Introduce innovation and leverage supplier expertise to build upon fit and product creation technologies (and tech at source capabilities).

 - Leverage internal data and external data to strengthen the technical design process;  combining insights to  inform technical decision making. 

 - Prioritize talent development.

 - Partner with the Head of Product Development and Sourcing to evolve team structure, ways of working, external partnerships, etc., enabling accelerated growth.



Qualifications:

 - BA or BS preferred.

 - 12+ years of experience in technical design and product development within Women’s apparel.

 - 8 years of management experience, setting strategies and leading teams.

 - Growth mindset.

 - Peti

source_jobs.title: Merchandise Planner
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

The Role:

The Merchandise Planner plays a pivotal role in driving the success of our merchandise assortment, focusing on maximizing sales and profitability. As the first hire in our planning organization, you will have the unique opportunity to shape the foundation of our planning processes. This role combines strategic planning with operational execution, optimizing inventory levels to enhance margin and fuel growth. The inventory guru thrives on innovation, optimization, and problem-solving, always seeking new ways to propel the business forward and meet customer demands.

 

Your Responsibilities:

 Inventory Management and Merchandise Planning

 - Take charge of strategic company planning and inventory planning to drive sales, manage inventory turnover, and exceed financial targets in your designated area.

 - Design innovative inventory strategies that effectively balance new and core merchandise, while supporting in-stock and turnover objectives within the company budget.

 - Determine optimal order quantities for core items and develop flow strategies, collaborating with vendors and merchandising to ensure timely and accurate execution of purchase orders.

 - Analyze historical data to extract valuable insights on product performance, pricing strategies, and inventory trends, applying these learnings to inform future seasons.

 - Accurately forecast sales and inventory needs, crafting strategies that maximize opportunities and minimize risks.

 - Maintain the planning system with precise parameters to enhance forecast accuracy, optimize receipt flow, and improve algorithm performance.

 - Develop and implement new or enhanced forecasting models that provide greater visibility into business performance and drive informed decision-making.



Who you are:

Qualifications:

 - BA or BS preferred.

 - 4+  consecutive years of Merchandising Planning experience.

Skills:

 - Data driven individual with a curious, entrepreneurial mindset.  Able to take initiative to start work quickly to meet deadlines.

 - Effective problem solver, with a hands-on “get things done” mentality. You are eager and able to solve challenges and tackle opportunities, identify potential issues and hurdles, and develop ways to get around them. 

 - Excellent quantitative and data analysis skills. You’re energized by data and are always looking to uncover insights to develop new ideas, test hypotheses, and make key recommendations. 

 - Proven ability to objectively interpret data and extract actionable insights that drive the business

 - Effective prioritization and organization to stay positive and proactive in an ambiguous environment are a must. 

 - Strong writing, communication, and presentation skills.

 - Understands that different situations may call for different approaches, showing the ability to be fl

source_jobs.title: Staff Engineer, Full Stack (US, Canada, SF)
source_jobs.description:

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   


SHORT STORY ENGINEERING:

As a fast-growing startup with team members across the globe, we’re scrappy, data-driven, and deeply customer-obsessed. Our team includes engineers, stylists, product thinkers, and storytellers—all working together to make shopping feel personal again.

At Short Story, everyone is empowered to contribute ideas, challenge the status quo, and take ownership. We keep our decision-making lean and our feedback loops tight, because the best solutions come from those closest to the work.



Our Product:

Short Story is a wardrobe-as-a-service that connects petite women with clothing that actually fits. Behind the scenes, our tools enable stylists to deliver personalized recommendations, while our logistics and warehouse tech ensure fast, seamless order handling. With a mix of internal and customer-facing platforms, we’re building thoughtful, elegant systems to power our growth—and that’s where you come in.



The Role:

As one of the earliest engineers at Short Story, this is a high-impact opportunity to shape our technology, product experience, and engineering culture. You’ll work end-to-end across both customer-facing and internal platforms, building the tools our customers and stylists rely on every day. From designing resilient backend services to scaling design systems and refining order flows, you’ll play a central role in how we deliver style and customer happiness at scale.

At this stage of our company, a Staff Engineer isn’t just a strong coder — you’re a technical leader, problem solver, and multiplier. You’ll collaborate closely with designers, PMs, and operators, but also look around corners: identifying bottlenecks, shaping architectural decisions, and driving the engineering culture that will set us up for the next chapter of growth.



What You'll Do:

 - Design, build, and scale full-stack applications with technologies like Next.js, React Query, TailwindCSS, Node.js, and PostgreSQL.

 - Drive technical direction: influence architecture, make trade-offs, and establish engineering standards that balance speed and scalability.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with design, product, and operations to deliver solutions end-to-end, from UI to backend services.

 - Translate Figma designs into seamless, interactive experiences while ensuring backend integrations are robust and performant.

 - Use data to drive impact: A/B test, instrument, and iterate to improve user experience and business outcomes.

 - Multiply team effectiveness: mentor engineers, lead design reviews, and raise the technical bar.

 - Ensure quality and reliability: drive testing and automation across the stack (Cypress, Jest, React Testing Library, integration tests).

 - Own deployment and operations: manage apps on Netlify, AWS, or equivalent, and build CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Acti

source_jobs.title: Warehouse Supervisor
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

Short Story is seeking a Warehouse Supervisor to lead teams within our fulfillment center in San Francisco. Your mission is to help us build a well-oiled machine, driven by a customer-centric mentality. You'll lead the charge on managing a team of exceptionally talented, bright, and committed people. As Warehouse Supervisor, you will help us deliver our promise to our customers. You will oversee the receiving, handling, storing, picking, and packing activities within the warehouse, ensuring quality standards are met at every turn. You must be an exceptional people manager and operationally savvy. You will lead a large team of associates and several functional Team Leads. You must have the ability and vision to help us scale rapidly. Our ideal candidate is exceptionally detail oriented, and able to track multiple facets of the warehouse at any given time. 



Your primary responsibilities will be:

 - Ensure the warehouse operates at peak efficiency, with customer satisfaction being the primary goal, by supervising, organizing, directing, and training warehouse employees and establishing, monitoring, and managing operation goals

 - Support development of warehouse operations systems by determining product handling and storage requirements, equipment utilization, inventory management, gate processes, and shipping

 - Review and prepare workflow, manning and space requirements, equipment layout, and action plans while ensuring productivity, quality and customer service standards are met

 - Inspire, develop, and support your direct reports and warehouse associates

 - Connect your team to our vision and bring focus & intention to delivering on our stated goals

 - Manage the labor planning needs for your team to meet weekly goals through accurate planning for current and future hiring needs

 - Review daily performance data and analyze for trends and opportunities. Make informed decisions and respond to changing operational needs effectively e.g. staffing and process changes.



You will support some of these functional areas:

 - Outbound: manage every aspect of the outbound shipping process including picking, folding, and shipping orders. You will ensure that every detail is perfect in our Short Story curations. This team is also responsible for processing Client exchanges.

 - Returns: ensure that all returned items are of highest quality before being placed back into inventory to delight another Client.

 - New Merch: receive new inventory from our vendor partners by tagging, categorizing, and binning 100% of our new inventory.

 - Support: enable a successful operation overall by solving fulfillment issues, repairing and restoring inventory, and keeping equipment running smoothly. 



Minimum Qualifications:

 - You have a four-year degree or equivalent professional experience

 - You

source_jobs.title: Styling Team Manager (Remote)
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

Goal:

At Short Story, we have a Fit-First approach to Styling. As our Styling Team Manager, you'll be at the heart of this—crafting experiences that make getting dressed effortless, exciting, and deeply personal for millions of clients. Your goal is to ensure that we deliver a top-notch styling experience to our clients. You’ll be instrumental in making sure we earn our client’s business over and over again.



Role Expectations:

This is a fast-paced, high-impact role at the heart of our mission. The role requires solutions-oriented thinking, adept management of a sizable remote team, and strong quantitative skills. This role can be demanding. You’ll be expected to push your team to continuously improve. You’ll not only identify issues, but more importantly, design and implement solutions. There’s a high level of collaboration with other functions. You'll also use your analytic ability to interpret data and make smart decisions.


Responsibilities:

 - Deliver a Delightful Client Experience - Train, coach, and inspire your stylists to provide best-in-class styling experience for our clients.

 - Manage, lead, and inspire your team of Advisors, Senior Stylists, and Stylists to ensure they are meeting/exceeding goals for individual performance, team success, and team morale

 - Drive Real Impact - Own Key Metrics and take actions to meet and exceed our daily, weekly and monthly performance metrics

 - Solve Complex Puzzles - From optimizing team performance to designing training programs that actually work, you'll tackle challenges that push your creativity and analytical skills to new heights.

 - Operations - You will own your team’s operational functions including shift scheduling, schedule compliance, box completion, time off/sick time, and timesheet approvals. 

 - Work Directly with Founding Team - Provide feedback on team performance, share learnings, insights, and ideas for improvements.

 - Hiring - Assess new stylist candidates, conduct interviews, and scale your team.

 - Training - Train, coach, and design programs for an engaging onboarding experience.

 - Team Building - build community within the Styling team and integrate Stylists into the broader company.

 - Collaborate Cross Functionally - with CX, Operations, Merchandising, Product Development, Marketing, and Engineering
   
   

Requirements:

 - Have led large remote teams of greater than 20 direct reports through ambitious transformations

 - Can switch effortlessly between strategic planning and tactical execution

 - Thrive in fast-paced environments where "good enough" never is

 - You have an eye for detail and quality

 - Have a strong analytical background and well versed in Google Sheets/Excel functions like pivot tables, vlookup 

 - You work well independently and collaboratively in a fast paced environment

source_jobs.title: Community & Growth Operator (Contract to Hire)
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   


THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Community & Growth Operator: an early-career, creator-native generalist to build our organic growth engine. This role focuses on what paid can’t buy: community, culture, word-of-mouth, and retention.



This is not a traditional community manager or social media role. You won’t just post content or moderate comments. Instead, you’ll help turn customers into active members, members into creators, and creators into advocates while flexing across growth and marketing projects as needed. Community is your primary goal, but you are someone who enjoys working across multiple functions. You might be hosting a Live one day, filming lo-fi content the next, helping on a shoot, or supporting a campaign launch. If you like variety, visibility, and learning by doing, then this role is for you.



This role is ideal for someone early in their career who has an innate desire to connect with customers and create an environment of customer love. You’ve probably already built something such as a community, a TikTok, a club, a niche page, a side hustle and want to do it at a brand with real scale.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


1. BUILD & ACTIVATE COMMUNITY

 - Participate directly in our owned communities (like our Facebook Group)

 - Spark conversation, engagement, and member-to-member interaction
   Identify highly engaged members and help elevate them
   


2. TURN CUSTOMERS INTO CREATORS & AMBASSADORS

 - Activate customers as content creators, advocates, and referrers

 - Support a small group of ambassadors and micro-creators

 - Provide prompts, frameworks, and light guidance for organic content creation

 - Track and surface top-performing community and customer content
   


3. CREATE ORGANIC, NATIVE CONTENT

 - Film and edit short-form, lo-fi, organic video content (non-ad, non-polished)
   Appear confidently on camera for Lives, BTS, and community moments

 - Repurpose live and community moments into reusable content
   


4. HOST & SUPPORT LIVE PROGRAMMING

 - Assist with planning and hosting live events (digital and in-person)

 - Bring energy, presence, and authenticity to audience interactions
   


5. EXPERIMENT, LEARN, REPEAT

 - Participate in test-and-learn growth experiments

 - Track basic performance metrics and iterate quickly
   Learn what drives participation, sharing, and retention
   


6. FLEX ACROSS MARKETING

 - Jump in as extra hands on shoots, campaigns, launches, or site updates

 - Support cross-functional projects as the team evolves
   


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - K-Factor: viral coefficient (how many new users each existing user brings in).

 - $0 CAC: Your primary goal is to drive the Customer Acquisition Cost as close to zero as possible through organic channels.

 - Active Community Rate: % of users who engage with the brand/community weekly.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Back-End (San Francisco)
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   


SENIOR SOFTWARE ENGINEER, BACKEND

What happens when your business model breaks the assumptions most e-commerce platforms were built on?

You build your own.

At Short Story, we’re rethinking how online shopping works: from inventory and logistics to personalization and fit, because the standard playbook doesn’t work for our customers. That means building systems that don’t fit neatly into existing patterns.

If you’re excited by designing systems from first principles and working on problems without obvious answers, you’ll feel at home here.


ABOUT THE TEAM

You’ll be part of a fast-moving engineering team building the systems that power our customer experience, warehouse operations, and data platform. We value engineers who take ownership, move quickly, and are comfortable figuring things out as they go.


THE ROLE

As a Senior Backend Engineer, you’ll design and build the services that power our core workflows, from order processing to logistics and internal operations.

You’ll own meaningful parts of the system end-to-end, making decisions about data models, APIs, and system behavior. You’ll work closely with product, frontend, and operations to ensure what we build is reliable, scalable, and actually solves the problem.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design, build, and maintain backend services that power core business workflows

 - Own projects end-to-end, from early design through production and iteration

 - Design APIs and data models that are clear, stable, and easy to work with

 - Work across systems (orders, inventory, logistics, data) to solve complex, interconnected problems

 - Make thoughtful tradeoffs between speed, scalability, and maintainability

 - Improve system reliability through monitoring, testing, and debugging

 - Contribute to engineering standards, code reviews, and documentation


MUST HAVES:

 - 5+ years of software development experience, 2+ Years of Python experience

 - 5+ years of experience with data structures and algorithms

 - Familiar with infrastructure, monitoring, and CI/CD practices

 - Familiar with the following technologies:
   
   - A Python web framework
   
   - Testing frameworks
   
   - Containerized workloads (Docker)
   
   - Relational database (Postgres preferred) and SQL

 - Focus on communication and documentation

 - Experience designing stable, scalable, and well-documented APIs


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Test-driven development (TDD) experience

 - Tech Lead for an engineering team of 3+ people

 - Proven track record running large-scale distributed systems

 - Worked in sprint-based environments


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS:

 - Generous Paid Time Off

 - Comprehensive health, vision, and dental care

 - 401k with company match (US)

 - Either in-person in San Francisco, or remote in US or Canada

 - Employee discount on merchandi

source_jobs.title: Senior Technical Designer - Tops & Dresses
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

The Role:

We are seeking a Senior Technical Designer, Tops & Dresses to lead fit excellence and technical development for our Tops and Dresses categories, ensuring every garment delivers best-in-class fit, consistency, and quality for petite clients. This role sits at the center of product creation - owning the technical fit process from concept through production. You will partner cross-functionally with Design, Merchandising, Product Development, and global vendor teams to translate creative vision into technically sound, production-ready garments. The ideal candidate is solution-oriented, proactive, and thrives in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.

Location: This role is based onsite at our headquarters in San Francisco, CA.

Responsibilities:

 - Manage the end-to-end fit process from first fit through bulk production

 - Oversee the development of block patterns and spec library to achieve consistent fit for your product categories carrying out seasonal product reviews

 - Develop and maintain Tech Packs throughout the product creation process. Ensuring detailed fit comments are created for Vendors domestic and offshore

 - Manage weekly fit sessions for your product categories

 - Analyze best and worst sellers for your product categories, driving continuous fit improvements

 - Manage time and actions. Ensure products are approved on time to meet delivery deadlines

 - Support with building out Technical Design Tools and driving key fit initiatives

 - Vendor Management and tracking monthly fit metrics

 - Collaborate across design, merchandising, and product development to refine style and fit (especially balancing comfort and proportions for petites)

Qualifications:

 - Bachelor’s degree in relevant field, or equivalent professional experience

 - 8+ years of experience in Women's Tops and Dresses technical design with strong category specialization

 - 5+ years of experience in patternmaking

 - A solid understanding of Petite Fit and grading.

 - Mastery of 2D Pattern Software, Google Sheets, Adobe Illustrator

 - Experience working with factories directly (overseas and domestic) and resolving real-world production challenges


KEY SKILLSETS:

 - Impeccable attention to detail required when creating and reviewing technical specifications

 - Analytical Thinking: Ability to diagnose fit and construction issues quickly, propose actionable solutions, and iterate effectively

 - Clear Communication: Skilled in conveying technical feedback clearly to both creative teams and factory partners across time zones

 - Customer-Centric Mindset: Passionate about delivering exceptional product fit and functionality for Petite clients, with empathy for their unique needs

 - Time & Project Management: Capable of managing multiple styles, calendars, and deliverables without compromi

source_jobs.title: Warehouse Manager
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   

The Role

Short Story is seeking a Warehouse Manager to lead operational performance within our fulfillment center in South San Francisco. This is a critical leadership hire as we are scaling our team significantly.

You'll sit between our operational leadership and floor-level supervision — driving accountability, enforcing standards, and building the systems and team bench that will allow us to grow without losing quality. You are a hands-on operator who is as comfortable setting KPIs as you are walking the floor. You've seen what "good" looks like at scale, and you know how to build it from the inside out.

 

What You’ll Own

Operations & Standards

 - Own operational performance across outbound, returns, new merchandise, and support functions

 - Align our functional areas around clear priorities and a commitment to quality.

 - Develop and improve warehouse systems, including product handling, storage, equipment utilization, and workflow design

 - Review daily and historical performance data, identify trends, and make fast, informed decisions in response to changing operational needs

People Leadership

 - Directly manage Team Leads and Supervisors; develop them into stronger operators and future leaders

 - Hold the team accountable to clear standards — you are not afraid to have difficult conversations and follow through on expectations

 - Build a culture of discipline, pride, and urgency on the floor

 - Partner with HR on labor planning to ensure the right staffing levels for weekly and monthly goals

Scaling & Process

 - Identify and eliminate bottlenecks as we expand our footprint and headcount

 - Build repeatable, documented processes that don't rely on tribal knowledge

 - Proactively surface risks and propose contingency plans before they become problems

 - Serve as the operational voice in cross-functional conversations about capacity, hiring, and tooling



Functional Areas You’ll Oversee

 - Outbound: Every aspect of the outbound shipping process — picking, folding, packing, and shipping orders. This team also handles client exchanges.

 - Returns: Ensuring all returned items meet quality standards before re-entering inventory.

 - New Merch: Receiving, tagging, categorizing, and binning new vendor inventory at 100% accuracy.

 - Support: Solving fulfillment issues, maintaining equipment, and enabling a smooth overall operation.



Minimum Qualification

 - 5+ years of experience in warehouse or fulfillment operations management

 - Demonstrated track record managing teams of 50–150 people, including managing supervisors or leads.

 - Strong floor presence — you lead from where the work happens, not from a desk

 - Experience enforcing quality standards and holding teams accountable to measurable outcomes

 - Proven ability to build or improve operational processes

source_jobs.title: Director of HR
source_jobs.description:
Our Story:

Short Story is an award winning, technology powered retailer dedicated to petite women 5'4" and under. Our mission is to create a seamless shopping experience for millions of petite women so they can dress with effortless confidence.

As a fast-growing startup, we're revolutionizing retail with a data driven learning system that leverages customer feedback to create tailored products. We've been recognized by top publications like Forbes, Fortune and are backed by leading investors who share our vision of building the world's next great consumer brand.



Our Values:

We're building a company with intention and purpose. Our culture thrives on data insights, exceptional commitment to fit, and the urgency to move quickly on what matters.

 - ✨ Transform Lives. We're customer-first. Everything we create exists to give her confidence.

 - ⚡️ Demand Excellence. We deliver only our best work, in Fit and Finish. We obsess about details others overlook.

 - 💪 Embrace Challenges. We see obstacles as opportunities to invent better solutions.

 - 💡 Think Differently. We get a thrill from finding better solutions others haven't seen.
   
   


THE ROLE

Startups win by having great people, clear standards, and repeatable infrastructure. We’re looking for a strategic, hands-on Director of HR to lead and scale our HR function across our San Francisco corporate headquarters, South SF Fulfillment Center, and distributed stylist network.



Reporting to the CEO, you'll be the accountable owner to lead the HR function with judgment and follow-through to run it independently. You’ll partner closely with our People Operations Manager and business leaders to strengthen the systems that help our teams move quickly, operate consistently, and uphold high standards. This is a high-trust, high-impact role for an HR operator who is equally comfortable advising leadership, building scalable processes, and spending time with frontline teams. As we grow, you’ll help architect and lead the next stage of our HR function.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

You’ll own the health, compliance, performance infrastructure, and cultural alignment of three distinct branches, each with its own cadence and composition. Build simple, repeatable tools and routines that help managers handle performance, attendance, employee relations, documentation, and communication with consistency across functions:




1. CORPORATE HQ (PRODUCT, ENGINEERING, MARKETING, FINANCE)

 - Strategic Partnership: Be a thought partner to the CEO and leadership team on organizational design, workforce planning, and talent development.

 - Performance Systems: Build data-driven performance management, growth frameworks, and compensation benchmarking to retain top talent.

 - Process & Alignment: Standardize HR workflows that keep corporate teams aligned and moving quickly.
   
   

2. Fulfillment Center Operations (South San Francisco)

 - Oversee HR for the fulfillment team. Build and maintain strong HR practices for our hourly fulfillment workforce, from onboarding through day-to-day operations, using our existing HR and timekeeping systems.

 - Labor & Capacity Planning: Partner with the Warehouse Manager and People Operations Manager to forecast headcount, plan labor, and coordinate high-volume front-line recruitment.

 - Floor Presence: Regularly walk the floor, helping supervisors enforce rigorous standards in a culture of pride and urgency.

 - Employee Relations: Lead clear and empathetic employee relations strategies, navigating difficult conversations with good judgment.



3. Styling (Distributed Stylist Network)

 - Scaling: Evolve HR systems and processes for our distributed stylist network so they can support changing volume, team growth, and operational complexity.

 - Retention and Engagement: Improve how we recruit, onboard, develop, communicate with, and retain stylists.
   
   


MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - This role is 100% onsite, at our South SF warehouse and HQ (about 20 minutes

notes: Retail startup with a strong focus on technology and data-driven decision making.
